pairs.question: Who was Edward Lloyd Klepfer and what positions did he play in Major League Baseball?
pairs.answer: Edward Lloyd Klepfer, nicknamed 'Big Ed', was an American right-handed pitcher who played in Major League Baseball for six seasons between 1911 and 1919. He played for the New York Yankees (then called the Highlanders), Chicago White Sox, and Cleveland Indians. Klepfer also batted right-handed and stood at 6 feet 0 inches tall and weighed 180 pounds.

pairs.question: When did Edward Lloyd Klepfer make his MLB debut and which team did he debut with?
pairs.answer: Edward Lloyd Klepfer made his MLB debut on July 4, 1911, playing for the New York Highlanders (later renamed the Yankees).

pairs.question: How did Edward Lloyd Klepfer's baseball career intersect with World War I?
pairs.answer: Klepfer's baseball career was interrupted by his service in World War I as part of the American Expeditionary Forces in France. During his military tenure, he was exposed to gas at one point.

pairs.question: What were Edward Lloyd Klepfer's pitching statistics during his best season in 1917?
pairs.answer: In 1917, Klepfer had his most productive season, posting a record of 14 wins and 4 losses with a 2.37 earned run average (ERA) over 213 innings pitched.

pairs.question: What were Edward Lloyd Klepfer's career pitching statistics?
pairs.answer: Klepfer finished his MLB career with a 22–17 win-loss record and a 2.98 ERA across 98 games.

pairs.question: How poor of a hitter was Edward Lloyd Klepfer compared to other MLB players?
pairs.answer: Klepfer is historically notable for being one of the worst hitters in MLB history. In 125 career at-bats, he managed only 6 hits for a batting average of .048.

pairs.question: What did Edward Lloyd Klepfer do after retiring from baseball?
pairs.answer: After retiring from baseball, Klepfer worked as an independent oil operator and later joined C.W. Titus, an oilman in Tulsa, Oklahoma, in 1946.

pairs.question: Where and when did Edward Lloyd Klepfer die?
pairs.answer: Edward Lloyd Klepfer died on August 9, 1950, in a hospital in Tulsa, Oklahoma. He was buried at Rose Hill Cemetery.
